verdict_reasoning: LYN is a Src-family non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via dual N-terminal lipid modifications (myristoylation at Gly2 and palmitoylation at Cys3). These modifications tether LYN exclusively to the cytoplasmic face of the PM. No extracellular domain or transmembrane segment exists. Pre-no checklist: no cell-surface-directed antibody/ADC/CAR-T program targets the LYN protein body from outside; it is not a TM shedding substrate; no covalent/wash-resistant TM-partner tethering on the outer leaflet is documented; no documented outer-leaflet PM pool under any condition (cancer, cell death, activation, or germline); not a lysosomal TM protein. All five contextual buckets are ruled out: no cell-state ecto-pool, no tissue-restricted outer-leaflet display, no lysosomal exocytosis route, no dual inner/outer-leaflet localization, no stable surface attachment to a TM partner.
